Composition.Programmer 1:
Implement the backend code in app.py
- Develop the backend using Python and Flask.
- The website should be accessible via local port 5000, with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard).
- Implement routing for all frontend pages, ensuring each route corresponds to the frontend code.
- Handle file read and write operations on all data files (jobs.txt, companies.txt, categories.txt, applications.txt, resumes.txt, job_categories.txt) starting from the first line, using plain text without encryption.
- Support all functionalities  browsing job postings, submitting resumes, tracking applications, viewing company profiles, managing job applications, and searching.
- Frontend code files to be supported by backend routes 
  - dashboard.html
  - job_listings.html
  - job_details.html
  - application_form.html
  - application_tracking.html
  - companies_directory.html
  - company_profile.html
  - resume_management.html
  - search_results.html
- Ensure no authentication is required; all features are directly accessible.
Composition.Programmer 2:
Develop all frontend pages and static assets
- Complete the frontend code for all nine pages listed in the requirements.
- All URLs in the frontend code should be routes defined in the backend, not direct HTML file names.
- Implement UI elements and interactivity as specified, including buttons, inputs, dropdowns, tables, and div containers with the given IDs.
- Implement client-side form handling, navigation, filtering, and search functionalities.
- Develop and include static assets such as CSS (styles.css) and JavaScript (scripts.js) files to support styling and client-side logic.
- Frontend pages to implement 
  - dashboard.html
  - job_listings.html
  - job_details.html
  - application_form.html
  - application_tracking.html
  - companies_directory.html
  - company_profile.html
  - resume_management.html
  - search_results.html

Composition.Programmer 1:
Test whether the website can be accessed through local port 5000. In this test, you only need to test whether the first page of the website can be accessed through the local 5000 port.
Task 2  Test whether the first page of the website loads correctly and whether basic navigation works based on the example data provided in the Task.
Task 3  Test the elements and integrity of all pages. This includes verifying the presence and correctness of all specified elements on each page as per the requirements

background_prompt: EvoMAC is a software company powered by multiple intelligent agents, such as chief executive officer, chief human resources officer, chief product officer, chief technology officer, etc, with a multi-agent organizational structure and the mission of 'changing the digital world through programming'.
